read_plan.preview: Shipping in [[Solumora]] operates within rigid seasonal constraints shaped by weather patterns, river conditions, and maritime hazards. The profitability and viability of shipping depend entirely on understanding and wor

**Coastal shipping** and **river transport** operate on different seasonal calendars because they respond to different environmental pressures. Coastal operations face storm seasonality; river operations face water-level

**Storm Complexity (Thawmark through Plantmoon):**
Food systems depend on agricultural workers, fishers, and herders.
The [[Maren Valley]] produces [[Terravelle]]'s primary grain supply through work by 20,000-30,000 seasonal and permanent agricultural workers.
Coastal fishing employs 1000-2000 active fishers and 2000-3000 processing workers supporting maritime-based food production.
Mountain herding (goat, sheep) and lowland dairy operations employ 500-1000 workers producing milk, cheese, and meat.

read_plan.section: Urban Labor Surge and Slack Cycles
read_plan.preview: Urban employment in [[Solumora]] cities is not steady work. It is seasonal labor compressed into intense periods interrupted by weeks or months of minimal income and high unemployment. Understanding urban seasonal labor
Urban labor demand follows agricultural and trade cycles rather than independent urban rhythms.
**Duration:** 6-8 weeks of maximum employment availability
**Duration:** 8-10 weeks of moderate employment

**[[Terravelle]] Cycle (Temperate Northern Agriculture):**

top_pages.id: Food Labor Housing and Household Survival
top_pages.path: content/Food Labor Housing and Household Survival.md
top_pages.title: Household Income and Food Budget

read_plan.preview: The fundamental equation determining food security.

**Household income:** Typical working-class household (two adults, two children) earning 8-15 Watts per person daily across adult earners: 16-30 Watts daily, ~450-700 Watts weekly, ~2300-3600 Watts annually.
Food budget adequacy depends on seasonal employment patterns.
**Surplus accumulation (autumn):** During harvest peak (abundance + peak income), households buy additional grain (if income permits) and preserve goods. Goal: build 4-8 week buffer for winter.
